notes: The 3 correctness assertions (root cause identification, correct fix, evidence explanation) pass 100% in both configurations -- Claude finds the right bug regardless of skill
The 2 process assertions (multiple hypotheses, proposed experiments) pass 100% with skill and 0% without -- this is the skill's primary differentiator
With-skill runs use ~16% more tokens and take ~28% longer, which is the expected overhead of the structured methodology
For these test cases (medium-difficulty bugs), both configurations reach the correct fix -- the skill's value would be more apparent on harder bugs where the direct approach fails
The skill consistently produces the 'mode switch announcement' and structured H1/H2/H3 format as instructed
